You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 2aedbd4d).

## Issue to Resolve

Fixing issue #1765 with array syntax annotations
It seemed simplest to always force `@[...]` annotations onto separate lines.

Closes #1765 

## Description

Added separate check for `ANNOTATION` elements.

## Checklist

<!-- Following checklist maybe skipped in some cases -->
- [x] PR description added
- [x] tests are added
- [x] KtLint has been applied on source code itself and violations are fixed
- [ ] [documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/) is updated
- [x] `CHANGELOG.md` is updated

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/AnnotationRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION
  5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.ANNOTATION

  9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT
 10â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST

 19â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 20â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.nextCodeLeaf
 21â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.nextLeaf
 22â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.prevLeaf
 23â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 24â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 25â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 26â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 27â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 28â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.descriptors.annotations.AnnotationUseSiteTarget
 29â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 30â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 31â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 32â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.safeAs
 33â†’
 34â†’/**
 35â†’ * Ensures multiple annotations are not on the same line as the annotated declaration. Also ensures that annotations
 36â†’ * with parameters are placed on separate lines.
 37â†’ *
 38â†’ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 39â†’ *
 40â†’ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules. Moved since
 41â†’ */
 42â†’public class AnnotationRule : Rule("annotation") {
 43â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 44â†’        node: ASTNode,
 45â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 46â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 47â†’    ) {
 48â†’        when (node.elementType) {
 49â†’            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
 50â†’                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 51â†’            }
 52â†’            ANNOTATION_ENTRY ->
 53â†’                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 54â†’        }
 55â†’    }
 56â†’
 57â†’    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
 58â†’        node: ASTNode,
 59â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 60â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 61â†’    ) {
 62â†’        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
 63â†’
 64â†’        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
 65â†’            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
 66â†’            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")
 67â†’            !node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
 68â†’            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
 69â†’        ) {
 70â†’            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 71â†’        }
 72â†’
 73â†’        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
 74â†’            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
 75â†’        ) {
 76â†’            checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 77â†’        }
 78â†’
 79â†’        if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
 80â†’            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
 81â†’            node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
 82â†’        ) {
 83â†’            checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 84â†’        }
 85â†’    }
 86â†’
 87â†’    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
 88â†’        node: ASTNode,
 89â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 90â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 91â†’    ) {
 92â†’        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
 93â†’            emit(
 94â†’                node.startOffset,
 95â†’                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
 96â†’                true,
 97â†’            )
 98â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
 99â†’                node
100â†’                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
101â†’                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
102â†’            }
103â†’        }
104â†’
105â†’        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
106â†’            emit(
107â†’                node.startOffset,
108â†’                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
109â†’                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
110â†’                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
111â†’                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
112â†’                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
113â†’                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
114â†’            )
115â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
116â†’                node
117â†’                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
118â†’                    .nextLeaf()
119â†’                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
120â†’                    ?.let {
121â†’                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
122â†’                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
123â†’                        } else {
124â†’                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
125â†’                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
126â†’                            )
127â†’                        }
128â†’                    }
129â†’            }
130â†’        }
131â†’    }
132â†’
133â†’    private fun checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(
134â†’        node: ASTNode,
135â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
136â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
137â†’    ) {
138â†’        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
139â†’            val noAnnotationWithParameters =
140â†’                node
141â†’                    .siblings(forward = false)
142â†’                    .none { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
143â†’            if (noAnnotationWithParameters) {
144â†’                emit(
145â†’                    node.treeParent.startOffset,
146â†’                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
147â†’                    true,
148â†’                )
149â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
150â†’                    node
151â†’                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
152â†’                        .nextCodeLeaf()
153â†’                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
154â†’                }
155â†’            }
156â†’        }
157â†’    }
158â†’
159â†’    private fun checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
160â†’        node: ASTNode,
161â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
162â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
163â†’    ) {
164â†’        val isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList =
165â†’            node
166â†’                .siblings(forward = true)
167â†’                .any { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
168â†’        val isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine =
169â†’            node
170â†’                .siblings(forward = false)
171â†’                .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
172â†’        if (isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList || isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine) {
173â†’            emit(
174â†’                node.startOffset,
175â†’                "Annotation must be placed on separate line",
176â†’                true,
177â†’            )
178â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
179â†’                node
180â†’                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
181â†’                    .nextLeaf()
182â†’                    ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
183â†’            }
184â†’        }
185â†’    }
186â†’
187â†’    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() =
188â†’        getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
189â†’
190â†’    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
191â†’        psi
192â†’            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
193â†’            ?.useSiteTarget
194â†’            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
195â†’
196â†’    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() =
197â†’        getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
198â†’
199â†’    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
200â†’        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
201â†’            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
202â†’
203â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
204â†’        treeParent
205â†’            .children()
206â†’            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
207â†’            .let { it == this }
208â†’
209â†’    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
210â†’        siblings(forward = false).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
211â†’
212â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
213â†’        siblings(forward = false)
214â†’            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
215â†’            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
216â†’
217â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
218â†’        siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
219â†’
220â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
221â†’        siblings(forward = true)
222â†’            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
223â†’            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
224â†’
225â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() =
226â†’        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
227â†’            .leaves(forward = true)
228â†’            .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() }
229â†’            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
230â†’
231â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
232â†’        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
233â†’            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
234â†’        } else {
235â†’            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
236â†’        }
237â†’
238â†’    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
239â†’        node: ASTNode,
240â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
241â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
242â†’    ) {
243â†’        node
244â†’            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
245â†’            .nextCodeLeaf()
246â†’            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
247â†’                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
248â†’
249â†’                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
250â†’                    emit(
251â†’                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
252â†’                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
253â†’                        true,
254â†’                    )
255â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {

257â†’                    }
258â†’                }

261â†’
262â†’    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
263â†’        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = modifierListRoot.treeParent.treePrev as? PsiWhiteSpace
264â†’        // If there is no whitespace before the annotation, the annotation is the first
265â†’        // text in the file
266â†’        val newLineWithIndent = nodeBeforeAnnotations?.text ?: "\n"
267â†’        return if (newLineWithIndent.contains('\n')) {
268â†’            // Make sure we only insert a single newline
269â†’            newLineWithIndent.substring(newLineWithIndent.lastIndexOf('\n'))
270â†’        } else {
271â†’            newLineWithIndent
272â†’        }
273â†’    }
274â†’}
275â†’

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’### Removed
 10â†’
 11â†’### Fixed
 12â†’* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 13â†’* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 14â†’* 
 15â†’
 16â†’### Changed
 17â†’
 18â†’## [0.48.1] - 2023-01-03
 19â†’
 20â†’### Added
 21â†’
 22â†’### Removed
 23â†’
 24â†’### Fixed
 25â†’
 26â†’* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
 27â†’* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))
 28â†’* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
 29â†’* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
 30â†’* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
 31â†’* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))
 32â†’
 33â†’### Changed
 34â†’
 35â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0` and Kotlin version to `1.8.0`.
 36â†’
 37â†’## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 38â†’
 39â†’### Indent rule
 40â†’
 41â†’The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 42â†’
 43â†’### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 44â†’
 45â†’In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 46â†’
 47â†’The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 48â†’
 49â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 50â†’
 51â†’If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
 52â†’
 53â†’#### Class relocations
 54â†’
 55â†’Classes below have been relocated:
 56â†’
 57â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
 58â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
 59â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
 60â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
 61â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
 62â†’
 63â†’#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
 64â†’
 65â†’This is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
 66â†’
 67â†’The static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
 68â†’
 69â†’Both `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
 70â†’
 71â†’```kotlin
 72â†’import java.io.File
 73â†’
 74â†’// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
 75â†’val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
 76â†’  // Define configuration
 77â†’)
 78â†’
 79â†’
 80â†’// Process a collection of files
 81â†’val files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
 82â†’files.forEach(file in files) {
 83â†’    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
 84â†’        // Handle lint violations
 85â†’    }
 86â†’}
 87â†’
 88â†’// or process a code sample for a given filepath
 89â†’ktLintRuleEngine.lint(
 90â†’  code = "code to be linted",
 91â†’  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
 92â†’) {
 93â†’  // Handle lint violations
 94â†’}
 95â†’```
 96â†’
 97â†’#### Retrieve `.editorconfig`s
 98â†’
 99â†’The list of `.editorconfig` files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting a given path can now be retrieved with the new API `KtLint.editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path>`. 
100â†’

content.text:
Now let me understand the issue better. The issue #1765 is about array syntax annotations like `@[JvmStatic Provides]`. Let me look at the test case in the test file:

Looking at the test at line 543-550 in the AnnotationRuleTest.kt:
```kotlin
@Test
fun `Given an annotation with multiple annotation entries then do not force wrapping of the entries`() {
    val code =
        """
        @[JvmStatic Provides]
        fun foo() = 42
        """.trimIndent()
    annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
}
```

So currently the rule allows `@[JvmStatic Provides]` on the same line as the annotated construct. The issue #1765 wants to change this behavior so that `@[...]` annotations should always be on separate lines.

Let me look at the current implementation again to understand what needs to be changed:

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION
  5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.ANNOTATION
  6â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.ANNOTATION_ENTRY
  7â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST
  8â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT

 23â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 24â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 25â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 26â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 27â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl

 29â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry

 32â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.safeAs
 33â†’
 34â†’/**
 35â†’ * Ensures multiple annotations are not on the same line as the annotated declaration. Also ensures that annotations
 36â†’ * with parameters are placed on separate lines.
 37â†’ *
 38â†’ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 39â†’ *
 40â†’ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules. Moved since
 41â†’ */
 42â†’public class AnnotationRule : Rule("annotation") {
 43â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 44â†’        node: ASTNode,
 45â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 46â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 47â†’    ) {
 48â†’        when (node.elementType) {
 49â†’            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
 50â†’                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 51â†’            }
 52â†’            ANNOTATION_ENTRY ->
 53â†’                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 54â†’        }
 55â†’    }
 56â†’
 57â†’    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
 58â†’        node: ASTNode,
 59â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 60â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 61â†’    ) {
 62â†’        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
 63â†’
 64â†’        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
 65â†’            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
 66â†’            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")
 67â†’            !node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
 68â†’            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
 69â†’        ) {
 70â†’            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 71â†’        }
 72â†’
 73â†’        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
 74â†’            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
 75â†’        ) {
 76â†’            checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 77â†’        }
 78â†’
 79â†’        if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
 80â†’            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
 81â†’            node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
 82â†’        ) {
 83â†’            checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 84â†’        }
 85â†’    }
 86â†’
 87â†’    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
 88â†’        node: ASTNode,
 89â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 90â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 91â†’    ) {
 92â†’        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
 93â†’            emit(
 94â†’                node.startOffset,
 95â†’                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
 96â†’                true,
 97â†’            )
 98â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
 99â†’                node
100â†’                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
101â†’                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
102â†’            }
103â†’        }
104â†’
105â†’        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
106â†’            emit(
107â†’                node.startOffset,
108â†’                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
109â†’                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
110â†’                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
111â†’                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
112â†’                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
113â†’                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
114â†’            )
115â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
116â†’                node
117â†’                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
118â†’                    .nextLeaf()
119â†’                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
120â†’                    ?.let {
121â†’                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
122â†’                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
123â†’                        } else {
124â†’                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
125â†’                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
126â†’                            )
127â†’                        }
128â†’                    }
129â†’            }
130â†’        }
131â†’    }
132â†’
133â†’    private fun checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(
134â†’        node: ASTNode,
135â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
136â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
137â†’    ) {
138â†’        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
139â†’            val noAnnotationWithParameters =
140â†’                node
141â†’                    .siblings(forward = false)
142â†’                    .none { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
143â†’            if (noAnnotationWithParameters) {
144â†’                emit(
145â†’                    node.treeParent.startOffset,
146â†’                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
147â†’                    true,
148â†’                )
149â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
150â†’                    node
151â†’                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
152â†’                        .nextCodeLeaf()
153â†’                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
154â†’                }
155â†’            }
156â†’        }
157â†’    }
158â†’
159â†’    private fun checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
160â†’        node: ASTNode,
161â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
162â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
163â†’    ) {
164â†’        val isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList =
165â†’            node
166â†’                .siblings(forward = true)
167â†’                .any { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
168â†’        val isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine =
169â†’            node
170â†’                .siblings(forward = false)
171â†’                .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
172â†’        if (isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList || isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine) {
173â†’            emit(
174â†’                node.startOffset,
175â†’                "Annotation must be placed on separate line",
176â†’                true,
177â†’            )
178â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
179â†’                node
180â†’                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
181â†’                    .nextLeaf()
182â†’                    ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
183â†’            }
184â†’        }
185â†’    }
186â†’
187â†’    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() =
188â†’        getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
189â†’
190â†’    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
191â†’        psi
192â†’            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
193â†’            ?.useSiteTarget
194â†’            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
195â†’
196â†’    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() =
197â†’        getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
198â†’
199â†’    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
200â†’        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
201â†’            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
202â†’
203â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
204â†’        treeParent
205â†’            .children()
206â†’            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
207â†’            .let { it == this }
208â†’
209â†’    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
210â†’        siblings(forward = false).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
211â†’
212â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
213â†’        siblings(forward = false)
214â†’            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
215â†’            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
216â†’
217â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
218â†’        siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
219â†’
220â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
221â†’        siblings(forward = true)
222â†’            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
223â†’            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
224â†’
225â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() =
226â†’        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
227â†’            .leaves(forward = true)
228â†’            .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() }
229â†’            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
230â†’
231â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
232â†’        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
233â†’            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
234â†’        } else {
235â†’            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
236â†’        }
237â†’
238â†’    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
239â†’        node: ASTNode,
240â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
241â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
242â†’    ) {
243â†’        node
244â†’            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
245â†’            .nextCodeLeaf()
246â†’            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
247â†’                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
248â†’
249â†’                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
250â†’                    emit(
251â†’                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
252â†’                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
253â†’                        true,
254â†’                    )
255â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
256â†’                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
257â†’                    }
258â†’                }
259â†’            }
260â†’    }
261â†’
262â†’    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
263â†’        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = modifierListRoot.treeParent.treePrev as? PsiWhiteSpace
264â†’        // If there is no whitespace before the annotation, the annotation is the first
265â†’        // text in the file
266â†’        val newLineWithIndent = nodeBeforeAnnotations?.text ?: "\n"
267â†’        return if (newLineWithIndent.contains('\n')) {
268â†’            // Make sure we only insert a single newline
269â†’            newLineWithIndent.substring(newLineWithIndent.lastIndexOf('\n'))
270â†’        } else {
271â†’            newLineWithIndent
272â†’        }
273â†’    }
274â†’}

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. The problem is that for array syntax annotations like `@[JvmStatic Provides]`, the parent element is `ANNOTATION` (not `MODIFIER_LIST`), and the current code at line 79-84 explicitly skips checking when the parent is `ANNOTATION`. According to issue #1765, we need to force `@[...]` annotations onto separate lines.

Let me implement the fix:

content.text:
The existing test expects `@[JvmStatic Provides]` on a separate line to have no lint violations. But we need to update this test since our change requires array syntax annotations to be on a separate line. The test is testing the case where the annotation is already on a separate line, so it should still pass.

Let me verify by running the tests again with verbose output:

{"file_path": "/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/AnnotationRuleArraySyntaxTest.kt", "content": "package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard

import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

/**
 * Tests for issue #1765 - Array syntax annotations should always be on separate lines
 */
class AnnotationRuleArraySyntaxTest {
    private val annotationRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { AnnotationRule() }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation on same line as annotated construct then report violation`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides] fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation on separate line then do not report violation`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation with multiple entries on same line as class`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides] class FooBar
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            class FooBar
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation with single entry on same line as annotated construct`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic] fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation on property`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmField Volatile] var foo: String = "bar"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmField Volatile]
            var foo: String = "bar"
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation with parameters on same line`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[Suppress("DEPRECATION") Deprecated("Use new")] fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[Suppress("DEPRECATION") Deprecated("Use new")]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }
}
"}

 10â†’
 11â†’### Fixed
 12â†’* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 13â†’* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 14â†’*

 18â†’## [0.48.1] - 2023-01-03
 19â†’
 20â†’### Added
 21â†’
 22â†’### Removed
 23â†’
 24â†’### Fixed
 25â†’
 26â†’* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
 27â†’* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))
 28â†’* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
 29â†’* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
 30â†’* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
 31â†’* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))
 32â†’
 33â†’### Changed
 34â†’
 35â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0` and Kotlin version to `1.8.0`.

 37â†’## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 38â†’
 39â†’### Indent rule
 40â†’
 41â†’The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 42â†’
 43â†’### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 44â†’
 45â†’In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 46â†’
 47â†’The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 48â†’
 49â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 50â†’
 51â†’If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
 52â†’
 53â†’#### Class relocations
 54â†’
 55â†’Classes below have been relocated:
 56â†’
 57â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
 58â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
 59â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
 60â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
 61â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
 62â†’
 63â†’#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
 64â†’
 65â†’This is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
 66â†’
 67â†’The static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
 68â†’
 69â†’Both `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
 70â†’
 71â†’```kotlin
 72â†’import java.io.File
 73â†’
 74â†’// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
 75â†’val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
 76â†’  // Define configuration
 77â†’)
 78â†’
 79â†’
 80â†’// Process a collection of files
 81â†’val files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
 82â†’files.forEach(file in files) {
 83â†’    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
 84â†’        // Handle lint violations
 85â†’    }
 86â†’}
 87â†’
 88â†’// or process a code sample for a given filepath
 89â†’ktLintRuleEngine.lint(
 90â†’  code = "code to be linted",
 91â†’  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
 92â†’) {
 93â†’  // Handle lint violations
 94â†’}
 95â†’```
 96â†’
 97â†’#### Retrieve `.editorconfig`s
 98â†’
 99â†’The list of `.editorconfig` files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting a given path can now be retrieved with the new API `KtLint.editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path>`.

Welcome to Gradle 7.6!

Here are the highlights of this release:
 - Added support for Java 19.
 - Introduced `--rerun` flag for individual task rerun.
 - Improved dependency block for test suites to be strongly typed.
 - Added a pluggable system for Java toolchains provisioning.

For more details see https://docs.gradle.org/7.6/release-notes.html

{"file_path": "/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/AnnotationRuleArraySyntaxTest.kt", "content": "package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard

import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

/**
 * Tests for issue #1765 - Array syntax annotations should always be on separate lines
 */
class AnnotationRuleArraySyntaxTest {
    private val annotationRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { AnnotationRule() }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation on same line as annotated construct then report violation`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides] fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation on separate line then do not report violation`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation with multiple entries on same line as class`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides] class FooBar
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            class FooBar
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation with single entry on same line as annotated construct`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic] fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation on property`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmField Volatile] var foo: String = "bar"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmField Volatile]
            var foo: String = "bar"
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)

class AnnotationRuleArraySyntaxTest {
    private val annotationRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { AnnotationRule() }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation on same line as annotated construct then report violation`() {

            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation on separate line then do not report violation`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test

        val code =
            """
            @[JvmStatic Provides] class FooBar
            """.trimIndent()

            @[JvmStatic Provides]
            class FooBar
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmStatic]
            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1765 - Given an array syntax annotation on property`() {
        val code =
            """
            @[JvmField Volatile] var foo: String = "bar"
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            @[JvmField Volatile]
            var foo: String = "bar"
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation using array syntax (@[...]) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

            fun foo() = 42
            """.trimIndent()
        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
